DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MARIN MANAGEMENT, INC'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS, OR FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES, REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS; PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND TO COMPEL APPEARANCE AT DEPOSITION; MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND ATTORNEY; AND ATTORENY FEES REQUEST. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. FURTHER, THE COURT ORDERED SANCTIONS AGAINST THE DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000 TO BE PAID TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. MR. Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-13-13 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Settlement on the record.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Sonia Wong #11288.

Stephen Fraser, Esq. for the Plaintiff: Marin Management, Inc. and with Witness John Manderfield.
Michael Heath, Esq. for and with the Defendants: Panjak Patel & Sam Patel and San Jose BKD.
Case settled, terms read into the record as follows:
Settlement amount of $55,000.00
Initial payment of $30,000.00 to be made within 10 days.
Balance of $25,000.00 by installment payments of $2083.33 each month starting on 08-15-12 until fully paid.
If fully paid, plaintiff to dismiss the action within 15 days after final installment (07-15-13).
Each side to bear own costs and fees.
Parties agreed to terms on the record, matter was deemed settled by the court, and was set on the CRC dismissal calendar for 08-13-13 at 10 a.m. in Dept. 610.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defts For An Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatoiries, Specially Prepared Interrogatories, Request For Admissions; Production Of Documents And To Compel Appearance At Deposition
Off Calendar Jun-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MARIN MANAGEMENT, INC'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS, OR FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES, REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS; PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND TO COMPEL APPEARANCE AT DEPOSITION; MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND ATTORNEY; AND ATTORENY FEES REQUEST.
Appearances: Stephen A. Fraser (332.2421) for plaintiff and moving party; Michael Heath (931.4207) for defendant and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Further, the court ordered sanctions against the defendant in the amount of $2,000 to be paid to plaintiff's counsel. Mr. Fraser to submit a proposed form of order. (Note: this matter was heard on the Law & Motion calendar). (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Deault Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PANKAJ PATEL Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. Defendants not properly served and proposed answer is provided. Any writs of execution based upon the judgment are quashed. Answer to be filed within two court days from the date of the order. Request for attorney fees and costs are denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Oct-29-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Stephen A. Fraser, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Marin Management, Inc.
John Manderfeld, President of Marin Managment, Inc., was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Panjak K. Patel, dba KPS Investment Co., also dba Howard Johnson, San Jose.
Default was entered against defendant on August 10, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on October 26, 2009.

Exhibit 1 - Hotel Management Services Agreement.
Exhibit 2 - Marin Management, Inc. Invoice Statement.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Marin Management, Inc., and against the Defaulted Defendant Pankak K. Patel, dba KPS Investment Co., also dba Howard Johnson, San Jose, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $36,475.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $5,338.84.
Attorney's Fees - $2,825.00.
Costs - $405.00, for a total judgement in the amount of $45,043.84.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
